name: tying a knot
description: Interactive coaching course for tying a knot compiled in real-time from webcam footage.

steps.name: Preparing and Aligning the Cord
steps.description: Hold the cord firmly with both hands, stretching it out to inspect its length and ensure there are no pre-existing tangles or knots before starting.

checklist.description: The cord should be held taut and straight between both hands without any twists.

failure_conditions.trigger_description: Holding the cord too loosely, which can cause it to slip or tangle prematurely.
failure_conditions.mitigation_instruction: Maintain a firm but gentle grip on both ends of the cord to keep it straight and controlled.

steps.name: Forming the Initial Loop
steps.description: Cross one end of the cord over the main body to form a clear, circular loop, holding the intersection firmly between your thumb and index finger.

checklist.description: Verify that a stable, open loop is formed with the crossing point securely held in place.

failure_conditions.trigger_description: The loop is too small or the crossing point slips, causing the loop to collapse.
failure_conditions.mitigation_instruction: Ensure you leave at least 3 to 4 inches of working end beyond the crossing point and pinch the intersection firmly to prevent slipping.
